title: Abdominal Wall Defects
authors: Shilpi Chabra, Jamie E. Anderson & Patrick J. Javid

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Abdominal Wall Defects?
Gastroschisis และ omphalocele คือ 2 surgical emergencies ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน NICU ซึ่งต้องการ multidisciplinary team ตั้งแต่ prenatal. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) Gastroschisis = bowel exposed, no membrane, right of cord; omphalocele = membrane present, midline, cord inserts into sac — อย่าสลับสอง diagnosis นี้; (2) Gastroschisis: outcome ขึ้นกับ bowel function; omphalocele: outcome ขึ้นกับ associated anomalies; (3) C-section ไม่ได้ benefit ใน gastroschisis — vaginal delivery preferred; (4) NIPPV หลีกเลี่ยงใน gastroschisis — bowel distension รุนแรงขึ้น; (5) Omphalocele + hypoglycemia = Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่; (6) Vanishing gastroschisis = bowel strangulation in utero → short bowel syndrome — preoperative recognition critical.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
แยก gastroschisis จาก omphalocele ด้วย anatomy, membrane, umbilical cord insertion, associated anomalies
จำแนก gastroschisis เป็น simple vs complicated/complex และ vanishing — บอก clinical significance และ mortality risk
อธิบาย prenatal surveillance, optimal timing ของ delivery (37–38 wk), และ delivery mode ใน gastroschisis
รู้ neonatal immediate management: bowel bag technique, positioning (right side down), NO NIPPV, cord length ≥30 cm
เปรียบเทียบ surgical options ใน gastroschisis: sutureless umbilical closure vs sutured primary vs staged silo repair
รู้ associated anomalies ของ omphalocele: chromosomal (trisomy 13/18), cardiac (50%), Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (6%)
อธิบาย paint-and-wait approach สำหรับ giant omphalocele: topical agents, epithelialization, delayed fascial closure
รู้ other abdominal wall defects: body stalk anomaly, bladder exstrophy, cloacal exstrophy (OEIS), prune belly syndrome
วินิจฉัย umbilical abnormalities: granuloma vs polyp, omphalitis, delayed cord separation → leukocyte adhesion deficiency
จำแนก urachus/omphalomesenteric duct remnants: polyp, sinus, cyst, fistula, Meckel's diverticulum
รู้ management ของ inguinal hernia ใน preterm: incarceration risk, timing of repair, GAS trial neurodevelopmental data
1. Framework — Gastroschisis กับ Omphalocele เปรียบเทียบ
ทั้งสอง conditions เป็น congenital abdominal wall defects ที่ herniated contents ต้องได้รับ reduction และ closure แต่มี pathophysiology, associated anomalies, และ management ที่แตกต่างกันโดยสิ้นเชิง.
2. Gastroschisis — Epidemiology, Pathophysiology, Classification
2.1 Epidemiology และ Risk Factors
Prevalence: 4.3/10,000 live births ในสหรัฐ (Stallings Birth Defects Res 2019); กำลังเพิ่มขึ้น — เพิ่มจาก 4.49 (2004–2006) เป็น 5.12/10,000 (2010–2014) (Mai Birth Defects Res 2019)
Most consistent risk factor: young maternal age < 25 years; risk several-fold ↑ vs mothers ≥25 yr

Other risk factors: short interpregnancy interval, illicit drug use (cocaine, amphetamines), prescription opioid use, alcohol, cigarette smoking, low body mass index, maternal GU infections during gestation
No gender predilection; higher incidence in Hispanic and non-Hispanic white families

Embryology: primary midline malformation of umbilical ring; herniation right of cord without sac; etiology not completely understood; rare family cases; no specific genetic etiology — genetic testing NOT routinely performed
2.2 Classification: Simple vs Complicated vs Vanishing
Simple gastroschisis: bowel appears healthy, no intestinal complications, herniated contents easily reduced; survival > 95%

Complicated/complex gastroschisis: associated with intestinal atresia, segmental or midgut volvulus, ischemic bowel, intestinal perforation, or subsequent NEC; 7.6× higher mortality than simple (Bergholz J Pediatr Surg 2014)
Vanishing ('closing') gastroschisis: defect closes in utero → strangulates bowel → intestinal failure/short bowel syndrome. 4 subgroups (Perrone J Pediatr Surg 2019):
Type A (15%): ischemic bowel constricted at ring, NO atresia
Type B (51%): intestinal atresia + mass of ischemic but viable external bowel
Type C (26%): closing ring with nonviable external bowel ± atresia
Type D (8%): completely closed defect — nubbin of tissue or no external bowel

⚠️ Complex Gastroschisis = Different Disease
Complex gastroschisis มี 7.6× higher mortality vs simple (Bergholz 2014). อย่าลืมว่า: (1) associated atresia อาจไม่เห็นตอน initial closure เพราะ matted bowel ดูยาก; (2) volvulus/perforation/NEC อาจเกิดหลัง closure หลายสัปดาห์ — simple gastroschisis at birth อาจกลายเป็น complicated ในช่วง early infant period.
2.3 Clinical Presentation และ Prenatal Management
Prenatal diagnosis: ~90% diagnosed by routine ultrasound; US shows extraabdominal loops of bowel WITHOUT covering sac; maternal α-fetoprotein elevated

Fetal risks: IUGR (common — US underestimates weight in gastroschisis); spontaneous preterm labor; IUFD 4.5–5.2% (vs 2.8% uncomplicated; mechanisms: oligohydramnios, cord compression, volvulus) (South Am J Obstet Gynecol 2013)
Prenatal surveillance: ultrasound every 1–3 weeks from 24 weeks; twice weekly NST from 32 weeks; MDT = MFM + neonatology + pediatric surgery

Timing of delivery: 37–38 weeks for uncomplicated gastroschisis; preterm delivery < 37 wk = ↑ adverse outcomes (Maramreddy J Pediatr Surg 2009); 2 RCTs found no benefit of elective preterm delivery (Shamshirsaz 2020, Logghe 2005)

Mode of delivery: vaginal delivery preferred — C-section offers NO benefit and ↑ neonatal respiratory distress (Puligandla J Pediatr Surg 2004); recent meta-analysis: no difference in mortality/morbidity (Kirollos Arch Dis Child 2018); C-section NOT indicated solely for gastroschisis
3. Gastroschisis — Neonatal Management
3.1 Immediate Delivery Room Care
Cord clamping: keep cord long ≥30 cm — preserves option for sutureless umbilical closure
Bowel protection: clear plastic bag from nipples to feet; minimizes evaporative heat + fluid loss; standard NRP resuscitation otherwise
Bowel perfusion: evaluate herniated contents carefully — if bowel ischemia/infarction suspected → bowel detorsion or emergency enlargement of defect by surgeon
OG/NG tube to suction; keep baby warm; right side angled slightly down (prevents mesenteric kinking + maximizes blood flow)
3.2 NICU Initial Management
IV access → IVF + broad-spectrum antibiotics
No NIPPV: avoids distension of eviscerated bowel (Pet Am J Surg 2017); ETT only if respiratory support truly needed
Bowel bag: keep closed until surgical assessment — avoid opening to prevent contamination
Gastroschisis Prognosis Score (GPS): validated bedside visual bowel-injury-scoring tool — distinguishes low vs high morbidity; helps initial counselling (Overcash Obstet Gynecol 2014)
3.3 Surgical Options สำหรับ Gastroschisis

rows: Umbilical cord used as biologic dressing; coiled cord covers defect; secured with bandage; skin heals in ~14 days
Bedside; often no GA; ↓ vent days, ↓ NICU stay, ↓ time to feeds (Chesley Am J Surg 2015); Midwest Consortium: ↓ anesthetics, ↓ antibiotics, ↓ SSI (Fraser J Pediatr Surg 2020)
Fascial closure in OR under GA; monitor intra-gastric/bladder pressure for abdominal compartment syndrome; prosthetic patch if needed + umbilicoplasty
Less common now; risk of ACS; one RCT: sutureless vs sutured — ↑ time to full feeds with sutureless (Bruzoni J Am Coll Surg 2017)
Hand-sewn or spring-loaded silastic silo sewn to fascial edges; serial reduction over days by gravity ± external pressure; delayed fascial closure when nearly reduced
Accepted option; reduction > 7–10 days → ↑ silo dehiscence risk; useful for large/complex gastroschisis

blocks.text:
Sutureless ≠ Always Superior — RCT ยังไม่ conclusive
Meta-analysis (Kunz J Pediatr Surg 2013) แนะนำ delayed primary repair อาจ improve outcomes เมื่อ selection bias ถูก eliminate. แต่ multicenter RCT ยังขาดอยู่. 2 RCTs (Pastor 2008, Poola 2019) ไม่พบ outcome difference ระหว่าง primary vs delayed fascial closure. Sutureless closure ปัจจุบันเป็น preferred ที่ center หลายแห่ง เพราะ bedside, ↓ anesthesia exposure, ↓ hospital costs (Wong Am J Surg 2020).
3.4 Outcomes
Uncomplicated gastroschisis: survival > 95%; hospital stay 4–6 weeks (limited by time to full enteral feeds)
Complex gastroschisis (Fullerton J Pediatr 2017): mortality 2%; need ventilation 95%; bowel resection 10%; NEC 5%; sepsis 9%; home PN after discharge 2%
Gastroschisis = leading etiology of short bowel syndrome in pediatric intestinal failure series (Javid Am J Surg 2010)

Long-term: normal GI function and neurodevelopment in most (Harris J Pediatr Surg 2016); malrotation present in nearly all but midgut volvulus risk only ~1% (adhesions prevent twist; Fawley J Pediatr Surg 2017)
Boys with gastroschisis: undescended testes common — ~50% undergo spontaneous testicular descent; no routine orchiopexy

Resource-limited settings: mortality very high from lack of TPN access; targeted interventions: prenatal diagnosis + early enteral feeding protocols + fluid resuscitation → improved outcomes (Miranda J Peds Surg 2019)
4. Omphalocele — Epidemiology, Pathophysiology, Syndromes
4.1 Epidemiology
Incidence: 1.5–3/10,000 births; stable over time (unlike gastroschisis which is ↑)
Risk factors: maternal age < 20 years OR > 40 years; maternal obesity; most cases sporadic but rare familial occurrence
Babies with omphalocele twice as likely as gastroschisis to have other birth defects (Lakshminarayanan Early Hum Dev 2014)
4.2 Associated Anomalies — Critical Knowledge

rows: Offer amniocentesis; trisomy 13/18 = very poor prognosis
Fetal echocardiogram MANDATORY for all omphaloceles
Triad: macrosomia + macroglossia + hypoglycemia; Wilms tumor risk → surveillance
Fetal MRI total lung volume prediction; PHTN → mortality
Cloacal exstrophy, pentalogy of Cantrell with cardiac defects

blocks.text:
Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome (BWS) — อย่าพลาด
BWS พบใน 6% ของ omphalocele newborns และ up to 10–20% ของที่ตรวจ prenatal (Corey Early Hum Dev 2014). Classic triad: macrosomia + macroglossia + omphalocele/umbilical hernia + hypoglycemia. สำคัญมาก: hypoglycemia ใน neonatal period — monitor glucose ทันที; Wilms tumor + hepatoblastoma risk → abdominal US surveillance ทุก 3 เดือน จนอายุ 8 ปี. Genetics consultation และ methylation testing.
4.3 Pathophysiology
Normal: embryonic disk folds (week 4–5) → body wall closes at umbilical ring; bowel physiologic herniation → returns 10–12 wk with rotation/fixation

Omphalocele = incomplete abdominal wall folding + failure of intestinal tract to return from umbilical cord; thought to involve embryonic dysplasia + malfunction of ectodermal placodes (Khan Semin Pediatr Surg 2019)
Membrane = peritoneum (inside) + amnion (outside) + Wharton's jelly (between); intact usually but may rupture → management shifts to gastroschisis-like pathway
Liver herniation: common in omphalocele; presence of liver = higher risk of other anomalies
5. Omphalocele — Management
5.1 Prenatal และ Delivery
Prenatal: anatomy US + fetal echo (cardiac anomalies up to 50%) + fetal MRI (lung volumes in giant omphalocele) + amniocentesis (chromosomal)
Delivery timing: preterm delivery NOT beneficial; risk of lung immaturity in setting of pulmonary hypoplasia (Porter Prenat Diagn 2009)
Mode of delivery: C-section indicated for giant omphaloceles containing liver — to avoid inadvertent rupture of sac; vaginal delivery otherwise acceptable
Delivery room: handle carefully — prevent membrane tearing; cover intact sac with nonadherent dressing or bowel bag after inspection
Respiratory insufficiency common in large omphalocele: pulmonary hypoplasia + pulmonary hypertension → may need ventilatory support; Baerg J Neonatal Perinat Med 2015: PHTN predicts mortality
BWS: monitor glucose closely — hypoglycemia may require supplemental glucose or glucagon
5.2 Surgical Management
Small-to-Medium Omphalocele (intact membrane, stable baby)
Elective primary repair: excision of membrane + reduction of herniated organs + abdominal wall muscle closure + umbilicoplasty
NOT an emergency if membrane is intact — time available for anomaly evaluation + stabilization
Giant Omphalocele / Sick Baby — Paint-and-Wait
'Paint-and-wait' = daily topical application to intact membrane → epithelialization over weeks to months → delayed fascial closure months-years later
Topical agents (US): silver sulfadiazine cream (Ein J Pediatr Surg 2012: 18-yr experience); povidone iodine solution; Manuka honey; cadmium-free alternatives
Baby grows with adequate caloric intake (enteral feeds usually sufficient); abdominal cavity grows → herniated organs reduce by gravity; lungs grow → better surgical tolerance
Thyroid monitoring: povidone iodine → iodine absorption → neonatal hypothyroidism (Whitehouse J Pediatr Surg 2010) — monitor TFTs

⚠️ Povidone Iodine ใน Paint-and-Wait → Neonatal Hypothyroidism
Topical povidone iodine ใน giant omphalocele → significant iodine absorption through immature skin/membrane → transient neonatal hypothyroidism (Whitehouse J Pediatr Surg 2010). Monitor TFTs (TSH, fT4) อย่างน้อยทุก 2–4 สัปดาห์; ถ้า hypothyroid → levothyroxine ± switch to silver sulfadiazine.
Ruptured Omphalocele
Attempt bedside suture repair; if impossible → staged silo placement + serial reduction → delayed closure (similar to gastroschisis staged approach)
Ruptured giant omphalocele: high mortality + morbidity — reduced abdominal contents may impair ventilation in setting of pulmonary insufficiency
5.3 Outcomes
Outcome primarily determined by severity of associated anomalies — not the omphalocele itself
Post-repair volvulus risk: up to 4% of omphalocele cases (Fawley J Pediatr Surg 2017)
Long-term morbidity: feeding difficulties, failure to thrive, GERD, inguinal hernias, chronic lung disease, neurodevelopmental + motor delays (Bielicki Children 2021)
1-year survival: up to 90% in isolated omphalocele (Marshall Obstet Gynecol 2015); giant omphalocele = ↑ mortality from pulmonary hypoplasia + PHTN
6. Other Abdominal Wall Defects
6.1 Body Stalk Anomaly (Limb-Body Wall Complex)
Rare, lethal: abdominal wall absent → peritoneal cavity open to extraembryonic coelom; umbilical cord short or absent (direct placental vascular connections)
Diagnostic criteria — 2 of 3: (1) exencephaly/encephalocele + facial clefts; (2) thoraco- and abdominoschisis; (3) limb defect (club foot, polydactyly, etc.)
NOT associated with chromosomal anomalies; scoliosis + incomplete pelvic closure present; prenatal US diagnosis important for appropriate family counseling
6.2 Bladder Exstrophy
Incidence: 3–5/100,000 births; boys 2× more than girls; part of exstrophy-epispadias complex
Open bladder = lower anterior abdomen; pubic symphysis separation; NOT a surgical emergency — transfer to specialized center
Immediate management: nonadherent dressing over exposed bladder mucosa; evaluate upper urinary tract (US) + pelvic bone separation (XR)
6.3 Cloacal Exstrophy — OEIS Complex
OEIS = Omphalocele + Exstrophy + Imperforate anus + Spinal dysraphism
Incidence: ~1/100,000 births; associated with 1p36 deletion
Anatomy: omphalocele cephalad + two lateral bladder halves flanking cecal plate (bowel exstrophy) + pubic diastasis + imperforate anus ± spinal dysraphism

Management: nonadherent dressing to exposed mucosa; staged surgical reconstruction complex; overall survival high but substantial long-term morbidity (anorectal + urinary dysfunction + neurologic impairment) (Versteegh J Pediatr Surg 2013)
6.4 Prune Belly Syndrome (Eagle-Barrett Syndrome)
Triad: (1) absent/hypoplastic abdominal wall muscles + (2) bilateral cryptorchidism + (3) genitourinary anomalies (megacystis, hydroureteronephrosis, renal dysplasia)
Rare; genetic etiology: 3% have HNF1β (hepatocyte nuclear factor) mutation; transcription factor for mesodermal/endodermal development (Granberg J Urol 2012)
Mortality: pulmonary hypoplasia; long-term morbidity: urinary tract abnormalities → end-stage renal disease
Management: multidisciplinary — urinary tract anomalies + cryptorchidism correction + abdominal wall reconstruction; ongoing renal monitoring
7. Umbilical Abnormalities
7.1 Umbilical Granuloma vs Polyp

rows: Epithelial remnant of omphalomesenteric duct or urachus
Small (<1 cm), moist, pink/red mass; serosanguinous discharge
Bright red, round — serous or mucous secretions

blocks.text: 7.2 Delayed Cord Separation
Normal separation: within 1 week (variable; antiseptic regimens → later separation)
Delayed > 2–3 weeks → suspect: immune deficiency (leukocyte adhesion deficiency — LAD), urachal anomalies, or infection
LAD: delayed cord separation + soft tissue infection (omphalitis without pus); CD18 deficiency → neutrophils cannot migrate to infection site; CBC: marked leukocytosis without pus
7.3 Omphalitis
Periumbilical bacterial soft tissue infection; severity: simple cellulitis → necrotizing fasciitis of abdominal wall
Incidence: < 1% in high-income countries; much higher in resource-limited settings; polymicrobial: Staph, Strep, gram-negative rods, anaerobes
Treatment: broad-spectrum antibiotics; invasive infections → surgical debridement
Serious complication: infection spreads up umbilical vein → portal vein inflammation + thrombosis → symptomatic portal hypertension years later (Fraser Acta Paediatr 2006)
7.4 Persistent Urachus and Omphalomesenteric Duct Remnants
Both structures normally regress. Persistent remnants present as umbilical mass or drainage. Type determines clinical presentation:

rows: Moist, bright red umbilical mass + drainage; NO response to silver nitrate → surgical excision
Open to GI tract (omphalomesenteric duct patent at bowel end)
Rule of 2s: 2% population, 2 ft from ileocecal valve, 2 inches long; may bleed (ectopic gastric mucosa); band → bowel obstruction
Drains urine from umbilicus; surgical excision + bladder closure
No connection to skin or underlying organs
Midline mass ± pain/fever if infected; excision required

blocks.text: 8. Abdominal Wall Hernias
8.1 Inguinal Hernia and Hydrocele
Mechanism: patent processus vaginalis → intraabdominal pressure → herniation of contents into groin ± scrotum/labia
Incidence: 1–5% of children; boys >> girls; much more common in preterm (Peevy Pediatrics 1986)
Inguinal hernias do NOT spontaneously close — tend to enlarge; repair soon after diagnosis in healthy infants
Major risk: incarceration → strangulation → intestinal ischemia; also gonadal compression. Nonreducible hernia = urgent surgical evaluation

Preterm: prone to post-GA apnea; timing of repair controversial (Sulkowski J Pediatr Surg 2015, Duggan Arch Dis Child 2015). GAS trial (Davidson Lancet 2016): awake-regional vs general anesthesia — no neurodevelopmental difference at 2 years
Contralateral hernia risk: increased after unilateral repair — but not high enough to justify routine prophylactic repair (Wenk J Pediatr Surg 2015)
Hydrocele: fluid-only in processus vaginalis; present at birth → may spontaneously resolve up to 1 year; communicating hydrocele (fluctuates in size) → treat as inguinal hernia
8.2 Umbilical Hernia
Protrusion through open umbilical ring; normal ring closes after birth (months to years)
More common: preterm + African descent; noticeable with ↑ intraabdominal pressure (crying); usually painless
Most close spontaneously before school age; incarceration uncommon early in life
Repair: usually delayed until 3–5 years or until symptoms develop; absorbable suture — mesh NOT routinely used
8.3 Epigastric Hernia
Small fascial defects in linea alba; preperitoneal fat herniates → palpable subcutaneous cap with small stalk through defect
Usually asymptomatic; repair delayed until childhood; symptoms: pain with exercise, growing lump
8.4 Diastasis Recti — Not a True Hernia
NOT pathologic: visible separation of rectus muscles with intact linea alba fascia; midline bulge between xiphoid and umbilicus when muscles contract → mistaken for hernia
No imaging needed; resolves with normal growth; NO surgical treatment in infants/children
9. NICU Practical — 'Call จาก Labor Room: ทารกมี bowel นอกท้อง'
Step 1 — ที่ delivery room: ยืนยัน diagnosis (membrane present? = omphalocele; absent = gastroschisis); cord ≥30 cm; bowel bag ตั้งแต่ nipple line; NRP standard — NO NIPPV
Step 2 — ประเมิน bowel perfusion: ทันทีหลัง stabilize — ถ้า bowel ischemia → pediatric surgery แก้ไขทันที; bowel detorsion or defect enlargement
Step 3 — NICU: OG/NG suction; IVF + ABX; baby right side slightly down; bowel bag ปิดจนกว่า surgeon จะ assess; GPS scoring

Step 4 — Omphalocele workup: echo (cardiac up to 50%); genetics consult (chromosomal panel ± amniocentesis); glucose monitoring (BWS!); fetal MRI lung volumes ถ้า giant; thyroid monitoring ถ้าใช้ povidone iodine
Surgical timing: gastroschisis = urgent; omphalocele = NOT emergency ถ้า membrane intact — stabilize first
Fluid resuscitation: gastroschisis มี evaporative fluid loss สูง; เพิ่ม maintenance IVF; monitor urine output + weight
Family counseling: gastroschisis → reassure survival > 95% ถ้า simple; omphalocele → explain associated anomaly evaluation จะกำหนด prognosis; genetics consult ทั้งสองกลุ่ม

Resource-limited settings: gastroschisis มี very high mortality ถ้าไม่มี TPN — early enteral feeding protocol + vascular access + fluid resuscitation protocol = key interventions (Wright Semin Pediatr Surg 2018)
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Membrane Present or Absent คือ Diagnosis
เมื่อเห็นทารกที่ bowel นอกท้อง: มี membrane = omphalocele; ไม่มี membrane = gastroschisis. ดู cord insertion: cord เข้า membrane = omphalocele; cord ปกติซ้ายของรู = gastroschisis. ข้อผิดพลาดที่พบบ่อย: ruptured omphalocele ถูก diagnose เป็น gastroschisis — ฉะนั้น look carefully for remnant membrane + cord insertion.

PEARL 2 — NIPPV Contraindicated ใน Gastroschisis
NIPPV (nCPAP, HFNC) ห้ามใช้ใน gastroschisis เพราะ pressurized gas ทำให้ eviscerated bowel distend รุนแรงขึ้น → bowel edema + ischemia ↑; reduction ยากขึ้น (Pet Am J Surg 2017). ETT intubation เฉพาะถ้า respiratory support truly needed. ถ้า baby breathe well → spontaneous breathing preferred.

PEARL 3 — Omphalocele + Hypoglycemia = Beckwith-Wiedemann จนกว่าจะ prove ไม่ใช่
BWS พบใน 6–20% ของ omphalocele newborns. เมื่อ omphalocele baby มี hypoglycemia → สงสัย BWS ทันที: ตรวจ macroglossia, macrosomia, ear creases/pits, hemihypertrophy. Genetics consult + methylation testing. Monitor glucose; Wilms tumor surveillance ทุก 3 เดือน จนอายุ 8 ปี. อย่าลืม cardiac echo — CHD พบ 50% ใน omphalocele.

PEARL 4 — Delayed Cord Separation > 2–3 Weeks → Rule Out LAD
Leukocyte adhesion deficiency (LAD) ทำให้ neutrophils ไม่ migrate ไปที่ infection site → delayed cord separation + periumbilical infection ที่ไม่มี pus formation. CBC: marked leukocytosis (often > 50,000) ทั้งที่ baby ดูไม่ well. ส่ง flow cytometry (CD18/CD11b). อย่าเข้าใจผิดว่าเป็นแค่ late separation normal variant.

PEARL 5 — Vanishing Gastroschisis = Prenatal Strangulation
Vanishing gastroschisis เกิดจาก fascial defect closes in utero ขณะ bowel อยู่ข้างนอก → strangulation. Prenatal US: defect 'disappears' + bowel นอกท้องน้อยลงหรือ absent → เป็น bad sign. Type C (26%): nonviable bowel → short bowel syndrome + intestinal failure; Type D (8%): completely closed. Recognize prenatal → counseling + plan intestinal failure management รวมถึง parenteral nutrition + intestinal rehabilitation ระยะยาว.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Bergholz R et al. J Pediatr Surg 2014;49:1527: Systematic review + meta-analysis — complex gastroschisis has 7.6× higher mortality than simple; separate analysis of complex cases essential for outcomes research.

Perrone EE et al. J Pediatr Surg 2019;54:60: Vanishing (closing) gastroschisis classification into 4 subtypes (A–D); Type C/D have worst outcomes including intestinal failure and short bowel syndrome.
Shamshirsaz AA et al. Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol 2020;55:15: RCT — elective delivery at 34 wk vs routine care in gastroschisis; no benefit to early delivery; supports delivery near term.

Kirollos DW, Abdel-Latif ME. Arch Dis Child 2018;103:F355: Meta-analysis of delivery mode in gastroschisis — no difference in mortality or morbidity (sepsis, short gut, hospital stay) with elective C-section; vaginal delivery recommended.

Fraser JD et al. J Pediatr Surg 2020;55:2284: Midwest Pediatric Surgery Consortium cohort — sutureless closure associated with less general anesthesia, less antibiotics, fewer surgical site infections, and decreased ventilator time vs sutured closure.

Bruzoni M et al. J Am Coll Surg 2017;224:1091: Single-institution RCT comparing sutureless vs sutured primary closure in simple gastroschisis — increased time to full feeds and discharge in sutureless group; supports need for multicenter RCT.
Kunz SN et al. J Pediatr Surg 2013;48:845: Meta-analysis — primary fascial closure vs staged silo; delayed primary repair may be associated with improved outcomes when selection bias is eliminated.

Overcash RT et al. Obstet Gynecol 2014;124:551: Factors associated with gastroschisis outcomes; GPS (gastroschisis prognosis score) validated as bedside bowel-injury-scoring tool for initial risk stratification.

Fawley JA et al. J Pediatr Surg 2017;52:26: Multi-institutional study — midgut volvulus risk in abdominal wall defects only ~1% despite universal malrotation in gastroschisis; intra-abdominal adhesions prevent twisting.
Corey KM et al. Early Hum Dev 2014;90:421: Frequency of anomalies in gastroschisis and omphalocele; up to 10–20% of isolated omphalocele cases have Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome on prenatal evaluation.
Baerg JE et al. J Neonatal Perinat Med 2015;8:333: Pulmonary hypertension predicts mortality in omphalocele infants; PHTN assessment and management critical in giant omphalocele.

Whitehouse JS et al. J Pediatr Surg 2010;45:1192: Conservative management of giant omphalocele with povidone-iodine — 18-yr experience; significant iodine absorption → hypothyroidism; monitor TFTs regularly.

Davidson AJ et al. Lancet 2016;387:239 (GAS Trial): RCT awake-regional vs general anaesthesia in infancy — no neurodevelopmental difference at 2 years; informs timing of inguinal hernia repair in former preterm.
Harris EL et al. J Pediatr Surg 2016;51:549: Long-term neurodevelopmental and psychological outcomes in gastroschisis cohort — normal outcomes in most patients; favorable prognosis for survivors.

Miranda ME et al. J Peds Surg 2019;54:1481: 25-year gastroschisis study in middle-income country — improved outcomes with targeted prenatal diagnosis + early enteral feeding protocols; TPN access remains critical barrier in resource-limited settings.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Newborn มี bowel นอกท้องข้างขวาของสายสะดือ ไม่มี membrane คลุม — diagnosis คืออะไร? immediate management ใน delivery room?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: gastroschisis. Immediate management: (1) keep cord long ≥30 cm; (2) clear plastic bag ตั้งแต่ nipple line ถึง feet — minimize evaporative heat/fluid loss; (3) standard NRP resuscitation; (4) NO NIPPV — distends bowel; (5) ประเมิน bowel perfusion — ถ้า ischemia → surgeon แก้ทันที; (6) OG/NG suction; (7) place right side slightly down (prevent mesenteric kinking); (8) NICU transport urgently.
Q: ต่างกันอย่างไรระหว่าง simple gastroschisis กับ complex gastroschisis? prognosis ต่างกันแค่ไหน?

ตอบ: Simple: bowel healthy, no intestinal complications, easily reduced → survival > 95%; hospital stay 4–6 wk. Complex: intestinal atresia / midgut volvulus / ischemic bowel / intestinal perforation / subsequent NEC → 7.6× higher mortality (Bergholz 2014). สำคัญ: (1) associated atresia อาจไม่เห็นตอน initial closure — matted bowel ดูยาก; (2) volvulus/perforation/NEC อาจเกิด หลัง closure หลายสัปดาห์–เดือน — simple at birth อาจ become complex. Vanishing gastroschisis = worst form → short bowel syndrome + intestinal failure.
Q: ทำไม C-section ไม่ถูก recommend สำหรับ gastroschisis? และ timing ของ delivery ที่ดีที่สุดคือเมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: C-section: meta-analysis (Kirollos Arch Dis Child 2018) + multiple studies พบ no difference in mortality, sepsis, short gut, hospital stay vs vaginal delivery; C-section ↑ neonatal respiratory distress. ดังนั้น vaginal delivery preferred; C-section ตาม standard obstetric indications เท่านั้น. Timing: 2 RCTs (Shamshirsaz 2020, Logghe 2005) พบ ไม่มี benefit ของ elective preterm delivery; preterm < 37 wk ↑ adverse neonatal outcomes; deliver 37–38 weeks for uncomplicated gastroschisis; IUFD ไม่เพิ่มหลัง 35 wk (South 2013).
Q: Omphalocele infant มี hypoglycemia — ต้อง work up อะไรก่อน?

ตอบ: Hypoglycemia + omphalocele = Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome (BWS) จนกว่าจะ prove otherwise. ประเมิน: macrosomia + macroglossia + ear creases/pits + hemihypertrophy + visceromegaly. BWS พบใน 6% ของ omphalocele (10–20% prenatal eval). Work up: genetics consult + methylation testing ที่ 11p15; monitor glucose closely → GIR ± glucagon; cardiac echo (CHD 50%); chromosomal (trisomy 13/18); Wilms + hepatoblastoma surveillance: abdominal US ทุก 3 เดือน จนอายุ 8 ปี.
Q: Paint-and-wait approach สำหรับ giant omphalocele คืออะไร? ข้อดี-ข้อเสีย?

ตอบ: Paint-and-wait = daily topical application (silver sulfadiazine หรือ povidone iodine) บน intact omphalocele membrane → sac epithelializes over weeks–months → delayed fascial closure months–years later (Lee J Pediatr Surg 2006). ข้อดี: ไม่ต้องมี early OR; เวลา evaluate associated anomalies; abdominal cavity + lungs grow → ลด ACS risk. ข้อเสีย (povidone iodine): iodine absorption → neonatal hypothyroidism (Whitehouse 2010) → monitor TFTs ทุก 2–4 สัปดาห์. Indication: giant omphalocele (defect > 5 cm หรือมี liver) หรือ sick baby ที่มี cardiac disease ที่ preclude surgery.
Q: แยก umbilical granuloma จาก umbilical polyp อย่างไร? รักษาต่างกันไหม?

ตอบ: Granuloma: granulation tissue (fibroblasts + capillaries); moist pink/red mass < 1 cm; responds to silver nitrate cauterization; most resolve spontaneously or with cauterization. Polyp: epithelial remnant of omphalomesenteric duct or urachus; bright red, secretes serous/mucous; does NOT respond to silver nitrate (สำคัญมาก! ถ้าทำ silver nitrate แล้วไม่ดีขึ้น → สงสัย polyp); requires surgical excision. ดังนั้น ถ้า silver nitrate ไม่ได้ผล → คิดถึง polyp/sinus → ส่ง surgical consult.
Q: Inguinal hernia ใน preterm infant: ทำไม timing ของการผ่าตัดถึง controversial? evidence อะไร?

ตอบ: Inguinal hernia พบบ่อยมากใน preterm (vs term); risk: incarceration → strangulation + gonadal compromise. ปัญหาของ early repair: preterm prone to post-GA apnea → risk of respiratory complications. GAS trial (Davidson Lancet 2016): awake-regional vs general anaesthesia — no neurodevelopmental difference at 2 years; แต่ trial ไม่ specific สำหรับ inguinal hernia timing. Consensus: repair electively soon after diagnosis in healthy term infants; preterm → individualize: balance incarceration risk vs GA apnea risk; มักรอจน 36–44 wk corrected age หรือจน stable.
Q: Cloacal exstrophy / OEIS complex: components คืออะไร? chromosomal association?

ตอบ: OEIS = Omphalocele + Exstrophy (bladder) + Imperforate anus + Spinal dysraphism. Incidence ~1/100,000; associated with 1p36 deletion. Anatomy: cephalic omphalocele + two lateral bladder halves flanking central cecal plate (bowel exstrophy) + pubic diastasis + imperforate anus ± spinal dysraphism. Bowel peristalsis → prolapse of terminal ileum + hindgut lumens. Management: staged surgical reconstruction; overall survival high but substantial long-term morbidity: anorectal + urinary dysfunction + neurologic impairment (Versteegh 2013).
Q: Prune belly syndrome: triad คืออะไร? genetic etiology? cause of death ใน neonates?

ตอบ: Prune belly (Eagle-Barrett) triad: (1) absent/hypoplastic abdominal wall muscles; (2) bilateral intra-abdominal cryptorchidism; (3) genitourinary anomalies (megacystis, hydroureteronephrosis, renal dysplasia). Genetic: 3% มี HNF1β mutation (Granberg J Urol 2012). Neonatal mortality = pulmonary hypoplasia; long-term morbidity = end-stage renal disease. Management: MDT — urology + nephrology + surgery.
Q: Delayed cord separation > 3 weeks ใน term neonate ที่มี periumbilical infection โดยไม่มี pus — ต้อง work up อะไร?

ตอบ: Classic presentation ของ Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency (LAD): LAD type I = CD18 deficiency → neutrophils ไม่สามารถ adhere และ migrate ไปที่ infection site → soft tissue infection ที่ไม่มี pus + delayed cord separation. CBC: marked leukocytosis (อาจ > 50,000–100,000) แม้ดูไม่ sick มาก. Work up: flow cytometry: CD18 + CD11b expression on neutrophils (severely reduced/absent ใน LAD). Hematology + immunology consult; HSCT (hematopoietic stem cell transplant) คือ curative treatment สำหรับ severe LAD.
Day 63 Preview

Avery Chapter 63 — Neonatal Gastroesophageal Reflux
พรุ่งนี้: Neonatal Gastroesophageal Reflux (GER) — หลังเรียน abdominal wall defects (surgical emergencies) วันนี้ เราจะเข้าสู่ functional GI topic ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน NICU: physiology ของ lower esophageal sphincter ใน preterm, แยก GER (physiologic) vs GERD (pathologic), conservative measures vs pharmacotherapy (antacids, prokinetics), evidence basis ของ positioning, และ ข้อโต้เถียงเรื่อง H2-blocker/PPI ใน NICU — สัมพันธ์กับ NEC risk.
